title: Hollandaise Sauce
tagline: The silky, lemony butter sauce that makes eggs Benedict legendary.
description: Emulsified butter sauce with egg yolks and lemon. The mother sauce that crowns eggs Benedict, asparagus, and salmon.
ingredients: 1/2 cup (1 stick) butter, melted and hot
steps: Whisk egg yolks and lemon juice in a heatproof bowl until slightly thickened.
Set bowl over a pot of barely simmering water (don't let bowl touch water).
Whisk constantly until yolks are warm and slightly thickened, about 2 minutes.
Remove from heat. Very slowly drizzle in hot melted butter, whisking constantly.
Add butter drop by drop at first, then in a thin stream as sauce emulsifies.
Whisk in cayenne and salt. Add warm water if too thick.
Serve immediately or keep warm over barely simmering water up to 30 minutes.
notes: Temperature is everything: Too hot = scrambled eggs. Too cool = won't emulsify. Keep it gentle.
Slow with the butter: Add it drop by drop at first. Rushing causes breaking.
Broken sauce fix: Whisk a fresh yolk in a clean bowl, slowly whisk in the broken sauce.
Blender method: Blend yolks and lemon juice, then slowly stream in hot butter with blender running.
Don't hold too long: Best served immediately. It doesn't reheat well.
